snippets.title: Compare train vs test error (generalization gap)
snippets.code:
from sklearn.datasets import make_classification
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score

X, y = make_classification(n_samples=500, n_features=20, random_state=0)
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.25, random_state=0)

clf = DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=None)  # can memorize
clf.fit(X_train, y_train)

train_acc = accuracy_score(y_train, clf.predict(X_train))
test_acc = accuracy_score(y_test, clf.predict(X_test))
gap = train_acc - test_acc
print(f"train={train_acc:.3f} test={test_acc:.3f} gap={gap:.3f}")
snippets.explanation: High train accuracy with a much lower test score signals poor generalization — the gap quantifies overfitting on training rows.

snippets.title: Depth limit improves generalization
snippets.code:
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier

shallow = DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=3)
deep = DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=None)

for name, model in [("shallow", shallow), ("deep", deep)]:
    model.fit(X_train, y_train)
    tr = accuracy_score(y_train, model.predict(X_train))
    te = accuracy_score(y_test, model.predict(X_test))
    print(name, tr, te)
snippets.explanation: Constraining model complexity often raises training error slightly but improves test error — better generalization to unseen data.

snippets.title: Random labels: zero train error, no generalization
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier

rng = np.random.default_rng(0)
y_random = rng.integers(0, 2, size=len(y_train))

mem = DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=None)
mem.fit(X_train, y_random)
print("train acc (random labels):", accuracy_score(y_random, mem.predict(X_train)))
print("test acc (true labels):  ", accuracy_score(y_test, mem.predict(X_test)))
snippets.explanation: The tree memorizes noise on training rows (often 100% train accuracy) but cannot generalize — test accuracy stays near random guessing.

snippets.title: Estimate generalization with cross-validation
snippets.code:
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression

clf = LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000)
scores = cross_val_score(clf, X, y, cv=5, scoring="accuracy")
print(f"CV mean={scores.mean():.3f} std={scores.std():.3f}")
snippets.explanation: Cross-validation repeatedly trains on subsets and evaluates on held-out folds — a stable estimate of generalization before a final test-set check.
